chunks.title: About Raven
chunks.text:
# About Raven Roberts

Raven Roberts is a first-generation scientist from Appalachia. She runs
Tardigrade Innovation LLC and operates Tardigrade Quantum Laboratory
out of Newport, Tennessee, where she designs, builds, and brings up
experimental quantum and astronomical instruments from scratch
alongside computational and theoretical research in quantum machine
learning and gravitational-wave physics.

chunks.title: About Raven
chunks.text:
Raven started building computers and writing code with her father at
age eight. She began dual enrollment at thirteen, completing two
associate degrees from St. Petersburg College with a 3.89 GPA before
moving to concurrent enrollment at Harvard Extension School and Roane
State Community College, where she has maintained 4.0 GPAs at both
institutions.

Her professional career began with two internships at Crayon — first
remotely from the US, then in person in Vienna for the summer of 2023.
The Vienna placement also gave her conversational German, adding to
her existing English and Russian. After Crayon, she joined Flomoney
in Singapore as their founding AI and robotics engineer, where she
shipped a legal RAG chatbot, a humanoid autonomous robot stack, and a
sign language translation system across one focused year.

Tardigrade Innovation LLC was founded in fall 2024 to give her work a
proper institutional home — a recognized entity for collaborative
research, a holder of patents, and the eventual seed of something
larger.

chunks.title: About Raven
chunks.text:
Raven's research sits at the intersection of three disciplines that
are usually kept apart: quantum computation, gravitational-wave
astronomy, and embodied artificial intelligence. The interesting
physics, in her view, happens at that seam.

The active research threads include a variational quantum classifier
for LIGO gravitational-wave signals (collaborative work with a UCSB
PhD candidate, running on real IBM Quantum hardware, with an arXiv
preprint in preparation), a Clifford geometric algebra neural
architecture with rotation and Lorentz equivariance native to the
representation, and exploratory work on lattice gauge theory
surrogates and amplituhedron geometry.

Alongside the computational work, she builds the instruments. The lab
in Newport currently has seven active rigs in various states of
operation, from an MPCVD reactor for NV-center diamond growth to a
4-meter L-band radio interferometer used for 21-cm hydrogen line
observations and Crab Pulsar timing.

chunks.title: About Raven
chunks.text:
The longer arc of Raven's work is to build a multi-modal research
institution in East Tennessee — a privately founded laboratory that
combines radio astronomy, gravitational-wave instrumentation, and
quantum materials science, anchored to the land by intention rather
than federal committee. Green Bank, West Virginia, is the closest
existing analog: a research site whose presence has shaped land use,
limited cell towers, and kept a particular kind of quiet alive in the
Potomac Highlands.

The mission has both scientific and political dimensions. A serious
research site in East Tennessee creates jobs that require real skills
and can be learned locally — welding, RF engineering, machining,
software — without requiring the people who fill them to leave the
region. It also functions as a kind of structural protection against
the development pressure that's been hollowing out Appalachia for a
generation. A 500-acre radio quiet zone with an active research
institution on it cannot be rezoned for condominiums.

Raven is open about why this matters to her personally. She is
Appalachian by birth, and the long-term goal of her physics education
is to come back with the credentials and the tools to build something
permanent. The physics degree, the LIGO connections, the national lab
relationships — those are not an exit from Appalachia. They are the
inputs to a return.

chunks.title: About Raven
chunks.text:
Raven is fluent in English, German, and Russian, with conversational
ability in a fourth language she has not yet committed to publicly.
She plays cello and jaw harp, composes dark wave and gothic punk
music on a Moog Matriarch analog synthesizer, and practices scientific
glassblowing with borosilicate. She maintains a varied collection of
exotic animals.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
# Raven Roberts — Curriculum Vitae

**Position:** Founder, Tardigrade Innovation LLC. Independent researcher.
**Location:** Newport, Tennessee, USA.
**Call sign:** KN4ABK (FCC amateur radio license).
**Languages:** English (native), German (fluent), Russian (fluent).

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
**Louisiana State University** — B.S. Physics. Matriculating Fall 2026.
Awarded the Academic Scholars Nonresident Award (competitive merit scholarship).

**Harvard Extension School** — Physics coursework, 4.0 GPA. Concurrent
with Roane State, 2024–present. Including Calculus I–II, introductory
Physics I–II, and Linear Algebra.

**Roane State Community College** — Physics coursework, 4.0 GPA.
Concurrent with Harvard Extension, 2024–present. In-person laboratory
courses to complement the Harvard Extension theoretical sequence.

**St. Petersburg College** — Dual-enrollment student from age 13, 2018–2024.
Earned an A.S. in Information Technology and an A.A., GPA 3.89.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
**Tardigrade Innovation LLC** — Founder and lead engineer. September 2024
to present. Newport, Tennessee.

Independent research firm at the intersection of experimental quantum
hardware, physics-constrained AI, and gravitational-wave data analysis.
The firm operates Tardigrade Quantum Laboratory.

**Flomoney** — Founding AI and robotics engineer. September 2023 to
August 2024. Singapore (remote).

Built a RAG-based legal document chatbot for lawyers, judges, and
paralegals; an autonomous humanoid robot stack covering locomotion,
navigation, and imitation learning (ROS2, Gazebo, custom multimodal
SLAM); and a sign language translation pipeline for ASL and ISL.

**Crayon** — Data and AI engineering intern. May 2023 to August 2023.
Vienna, Austria.

Computer vision, LiDAR processing, sign language translation, and a
NASA-adjacent battery-failure prediction project.

**Crayon** — Software engineering intern. January 2022 to February 2023.
Remote (US).

First production engineering role; ticket-driven development, code
review, customer-facing software at a real engineering organization.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
**VQE × LIGO classifier.** Variational quantum eigensolver classifier
for binary-merger gravitational waves. Trained on real superconducting
hardware (IBM Quantum's ibm_nairobi), targeting discrimination between
true chirps and glitch artefacts in O3 strain data. Active collaboration
with a UCSB PhD candidate; arXiv preprint in preparation.

**Clifford geometric algebra agents.** Cl(3,0) and Cl(3,1) equivariant
neural architectures with rotation, boost, and reflection symmetries
native to the network rather than imposed by loss-function constraints.
Released as the open-source `tardigrade_agent` Python package, with
plasma instability control via BOUT++ as the initial application
target.

**Topological knot invariant cryptography.** Concept paper exploring
knot invariants as the basis for post-quantum cryptographic primitives.
Speculative; in early drafting.

**Lattice QFT surrogates for amplituhedron volume estimation.** Combining
lattice gauge theory ML surrogates with positive-geometry sampling —
exploratory work presented at a LIGO convention.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
- **MPCVD reactor** for NV-center diamond growth, with Pfeiffer DUO 2.5
  vacuum and microwave cavity assembly.
- **Paul trap** for ¹⁷¹Yb⁺ ion trapping, with custom RF drive electronics
  and laser cooling optics.
- **Dual 4-meter L-band radio interferometer** targeting the 21-cm
  hydrogen line and Crab Pulsar timing residuals. SAWbird H1 LNA chain
  feeding Airspy Mini SDRs.
- **h-BN single-photon emitter** integrated with a whispering-gallery-mode
  optical resonator.
- **Chalcogenide memristor reservoir computer** with Cu₂S electrodes.
- **Earth-field NMR spectrometer.**
- **Scintillator glass synthesis** for cosmic-ray detection projects.
- **Van de Graaff generator build** (1.5–2.0 MV target) for 3D Lichtenberg
  figure production in polycarbonate.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
**Dual-modality GFET / rGO architecture for chronic wound monitoring.**
USPTO provisional patent application, lead inventor. Combines a
graphene field-effect transistor with an electrochemical reduced-graphene-oxide
sensor on a flexible substrate, with TinyML inference on a Nordic
nRF52840.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
- **AEI Hannover** (Albert Einstein Institute, Max Planck): positive
  response on a visiting researcher arrangement around continuous
  gravitational-wave searches and pulsar timing.
- **IonQ Chattanooga**: ongoing conversation regarding the EPB fiber
  quantum networking testbed.
- **GSSI / Gran Sasso**: ACME Trans-National Access proposal submitted
  to Marica Branchesi's group.
- Targeted outreach also active to ORNL (Dr. Huan Zhao, NV-center
  magnetometry), Sandia QSCOUT, LANL, EGO/Virgo, Cardiff GW group, and
  others.

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
- **LSU Academic Scholars Nonresident Award** (2026)
- **Amateur Radio License KN4ABK** (FCC, 2024)
- **4.0 GPA** at Harvard Extension School and Roane State Community College

chunks.title: Curriculum Vitae
chunks.text:
- Cello, jaw harp, and Moog Matriarch synthesizer (dark wave / gothic
  punk composition).
- Scientific glassblowing (borosilicate).
- Long-term husbandry of a varied collection of exotic animals.

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text: # Frequently asked questions

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text:
### Tell me about Raven's background.

Raven Roberts is a first-generation scientist from Appalachia, based
in Newport, Tennessee. She started building computers and writing
code at age eight, began dual enrollment at thirteen, and has
maintained 4.0 GPAs at both Harvard Extension School and Roane State
Community College alongside three years of production AI engineering
experience at Crayon (remote US and Vienna) and Flomoney (Singapore).
She founded Tardigrade Innovation LLC in fall 2024 and is matriculating
at Louisiana State University as a B.S. Physics student in fall 2026.

### Where did Raven study?

Raven completed associate degrees at St. Petersburg College (3.89 GPA,
2018–2024) starting at age thirteen as a dual-enrollment student. She
then enrolled concurrently at Roane State Community College and
Harvard Extension School, maintaining a 4.0 GPA at both. She is
matriculating at Louisiana State University in fall 2026.

### What is Raven studying at LSU?

Physics, B.S. She received the Academic Scholars Nonresident Award —
a competitive merit scholarship — and chose LSU specifically for its
gravitational-wave faculty and its proximity to LIGO Livingston.

### What languages does Raven speak?

English (native), German (fluent), and Russian (fluent). The German
came from her summer in Vienna with Crayon in 2023.

### Where is Raven based?

Newport, Tennessee, in East Tennessee — the eastern Appalachian region
of the state. She is moving to Baton Rouge for the LSU program in
summer 2026.

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text:
### What is Tardigrade Innovation?

Tardigrade Innovation LLC is an independent research firm Raven
founded in 2024. It operates Tardigrade Quantum Laboratory in
Newport, Tennessee, and serves as the institutional home for her
hardware builds, software products, patent applications, and
collaborative research.

### What is Raven working on right now?

The most active research thread is a variational quantum eigensolver
classifier for LIGO gravitational-wave signals, in collaboration with
a UCSB PhD candidate and run on real IBM Quantum hardware (ibm_nairobi).
The arXiv preprint is in preparation as of 2026. Alongside the
classifier work, the lab in Newport has seven active hardware rigs in
various states of operation — including an MPCVD reactor for NV-center
diamond, a Paul trap toward Yb⁺ ion trapping, and a 4-meter L-band
radio interferometer.

### What's Raven's research focus?

Her research lives at the intersection of quantum computation,
gravitational-wave astronomy, and physics-constrained embodied AI.
The interesting physics, in her framing, happens at that seam.

### Is Raven looking for collaborators?

Yes — actively, especially around the VQE/LIGO classifier work, the
Clifford geometric algebra agent, and the L-band interferometer's
pulsar timing program. The contact form on the site is the right
route for collaboration inquiries.

### Does Raven supervise students?

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text:
Not formally — she is herself an undergraduate. She does correspond
with other early-career researchers and first-generation scientists
who write to her, and is consistent about answering those messages.

### Is Raven taking on consulting clients?

She takes on a limited number of consulting engagements per year
through Tardigrade Innovation LLC, typically in quantum machine
learning, gravitational-wave data pipelines, hardware design, or
graduate application strategy. The contact form is the right way to
inquire.

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text:
### What's the connection to Appalachia?

Raven is from East Tennessee and intends to come back. The longer arc
of her career is to build a multi-modal research institution in
Appalachia — combining radio astronomy, gravitational-wave
instrumentation, and quantum materials science, anchored to the land
by intention rather than federal committee. The model in mind is
something closer to Green Bank, West Virginia: a serious research
site whose presence shapes regional land use and provides skilled
local employment.

### Why does this matter?

A serious research institution in East Tennessee creates jobs that
can be learned locally and don't require leaving the region, and it
provides a structural protection against the development pressure
that's been hollowing out Appalachia for a generation. Raven is open
about the political dimension of this.

chunks.title: Frequently asked questions
chunks.text:
### How can I contact Raven?

The contact form on the site is the best route. She reads every
message and replies inside 48 hours, usually faster. For students and
early-career researchers, especially folks from rural and
first-generation backgrounds — yes, you can write cold.

### How do I write a good cold email to Raven?

Be specific about what you're working on, why your work overlaps
with hers, and what you're actually asking for. One paragraph is
better than three.

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text: # VQE × LIGO classifier

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text:
A variational quantum eigensolver classifier trained to discriminate
true binary-merger gravitational-wave signals from glitch artefacts in
LIGO O3 strain data. The classifier runs on IBM Quantum's
superconducting `ibm_nairobi` processor — a real quantum backend, not
a simulator — and on PennyLane with the lightning.qubit local
simulator for development.

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text:
Most published work on quantum machine learning for gravitational
waves runs entirely on classical simulators. Running on real
superconducting hardware exposes the classifier to noise, decoherence,
and gate-level error that simulator work doesn't capture. The
preprint compares simulated and on-hardware performance directly.

The architecture also uses physics-motivated encoding choices —
amplitude encoding for the strain windows after whitening — rather
than treating the input as an arbitrary feature vector.

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text:
The preprint is in active preparation as of 2026. The collaboration
is with a UCSB PhD candidate; Tardigrade Innovation LLC is the
contracting party for Raven's contributions. The work is being
developed in the orbit of the LIGO Scientific Collaboration but is
not yet a formal LSC publication.

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text:
The same architecture has natural extensions toward exotic compact
object detection (sub-threshold signals from boson stars and
non-standard compact remnants outside the matched-filter template
banks) and binary parameter estimation. The Clifford geometric algebra
work in `tardigrade_agent` is a complementary architectural direction
that may eventually be combined with the variational classifier.

chunks.title: VQE × LIGO classifier
chunks.text:
- arXiv preprint (forthcoming)
- Code release alongside the preprint
- Cleaned strain dataset (subset, for educational use)
- Live demo on the Tardigrade site
